play.title: Don't Leak PII/PHI/Secrets
play.description: Exception messages or log statements that include raw response bodies, user params, or data that could contain PII/PHI or secrets.

pattern.context: raw upstream response body in rendered JSON or hash literal
error.try(:original_body) in hash values rendered to client
render json: with error hash that may include raw upstream data
vamf_body often contains raw upstream response
Ruby shorthand dumps exception#inspect which may contain PII from message

play.context_check: For `original_body`, `vamf_body`, or `render.*json:.*error` matches: 1. Read the full method and identify the hash being constructed. 2. Trace each value for raw upstream data (.body, .original_body, response.body). 3. Rendered to client (render json:, response_values): HIGH. Logs only: MEDIUM. Sanitized: exclude.

play.title: Preserve Cause Chains
play.description: Rescue blocks that raise a new exception in a way that destroys the cause chain. Main anti-pattern: raise "error: #{e}" which creates a RuntimeError and loses the original class and backtrace.

play.title: Never Use Bare Rescues
play.description: Rescue blocks without an exception class (rescue, rescue => e, or rescue Exception).
play.context_check: Rescue followed by nil, false, [], or {} return: MEDIUM (error swallowing).

play.title: Map Upstream Network Errors
play.description: Faraday error rescues mapped to InternalServerError (500). Upstream failures should map to 502/503/504.

pattern.context: blanket catch may be intentional in service layer

play.context_check: Faraday rescue followed by InternalServerError raise: HIGH.

play.title: Match Status Codes to Fault Ownership
play.description: Broad rescue blocks that return 422 UnprocessableEntity, disguising server errors as client validation errors. Also: error-status mapper methods that default to 422 in an else branch.

pattern.context: read the rescue clause above each usage
check enclosing rescue or error-handling method
check else branch in error-status mappers

play.context_check: - Bare/broad rescue raising 422: HIGH. - Error-status mapper with else defaulting to 422: MEDIUM. Trace what values reach the else branch.

play.title: Handle 401 Token Ownership
play.description: Upstream 401 errors blindly passed through to clients. When service account credentials fail, that's our fault (500), not the user's (401).

pattern.context: investigate credential ownership (user token vs service account)

play.context_check: Upstream 401 passed through without checking credential ownership: HIGH.

play.title: Handle 403 Permission vs Existence
play.description: 403 Forbidden returned for non-existent resources (should be 404) or expired tokens (should be 401). Leaks resource existence information.

pattern.context: check if permission issue or missing resource

play.context_check: RecordNotFound rescued and converted to Forbidden: HIGH.

play.title: Prefer Typed Exceptions
play.description: raise 'string message' in HTTP request paths. These become RuntimeError and are indistinguishable in APM.

play.title: Expected vs Unexpected Errors
play.description: set_error() called on 4xx exceptions. Only 5xx errors should mark APM spans as errors.

play.title: Don't Build Module-Specific Frameworks
play.description: Custom logging services, error handlers, monitoring wrappers, or error classification methods within a module. Use Rails.logger, StatsD, Datadog::Tracing, and ExceptionHandling directly.

pattern.context: check it's not a domain model
